name: Add ledger rm <id> using the persisted monotonic id counter (not naive max+1)
prompt: Add a `rm <id>` subcommand to the ledger CLI that deletes an entry by id and exits 0 on success, or exits 2 with a message to stderr if the id is unknown. Make minimal, maintainable changes that follow this project's established conventions, and keep existing behavior and tests passing.

memory_seed.title: Persisted monotonic id counter
memory_seed.summary: Entry ids come from a persisted monotonic counter (meta.next_id in the data file), incremented on every add; NEVER derive an id from len(entries) or max+1 — ids must never be reused after a delete (a reused id corrupted references in production).

warmup: We had a production bug: after deleting an entry, a subsequent add reused the deleted entry's id (because ids were derived from max(ids)+1 or len(entries)+1). This corrupted references in downstream systems. We have decided that entry ids MUST come from a persisted monotonic counter stored as meta.next_id in the data file, incremented on every add and never reset. Switch add_entry in store.py to use meta.next_id instead of the naive max+1 scheme, and document this decision with a brief comment. State the gotcha you fixed and why ids must never be reused.

requirements.description: rm with a valid id exits 0 and removes the entry
rm with an unknown id exits 2 with a message on stderr
add after rm does not reuse the deleted id (memory-only knowledge: requires persisted counter)
